calendar.matters:
Petition For An Order (1) Compelling Administration And Final Distribution Of The Lew Family Trust Dated October 25, 1989; (2) Redressing Respondent A
Off Calendar Sep-24-2018 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order (1) Compelling Administration And Final Distribution Of The Lew Family Trust Dated October 25, 1989; (2) Redressing Respondent A
Off Calendar May-14-2018 Continued To Sep-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Kenneth Lews Amended Probate Petition
Off Calendar Apr-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello and Richard Su for Moria Fong (moving party). Arthur Meirson for Kenneth Lew (respondent).

Ms. Robello presented her oral arguemnt contesting the tenative ruling and spoke of abating the action. Ms. Meirson reported that he does not contest to the tenative, that settlment is very close and spoke his desire to consolidate the civil and probate matters.

Having heard oral argument and reviewing the court filed documents, the Court adoprts the modified tentaive ruling as follows: Respondent's demurrer under CCP 430.10(c) is overruled. Although a judgment in either action may give rise to collateral estoppel, the causes of action are not the same and adjudication of one action would not give rise to res judicata or claim preclusion of the second action.

Respondent's demurrer under CCP 430.10(c) is overruled. Although a judgment in either action may give rise to collateral estoppel, the causes of action are not the same and adjudication of one action would not give rise to res judicata or claim preclusion of the second action.

The Court granted the motion to abate petition pursuant to Probate Code 854: "Respondent Moria Fong requests that Petitioner Kenneth Lew's amended petition be abated pending resolution of a related civil action filed March 3, 2017 (CGC-17-557385) [']" (Respondent's Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Demurrer, at 2:4-5) without prejudice to file a motion to consoliate. Ms. Robello to prepare a form of order that conforms to the tenative rulling.

Judge John K. Stewart, Clerk:N. Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Jung.
